definitions.definition: Organic compounds that have a carbonyl group (C=O) at the end of a carbon chain, in which the carbonyl carbon is bonded to at least one hydrogen atom.
definitions.example: Formaldehyde (HCHO) is an example of an aldehyde.

definitions.definition: Organic compounds that have a carbonyl group (C=O) within a carbon chain, in which the carbonyl carbon is bonded to two other carbon atoms.
definitions.example: Acetone (CH3COCH3) is an example of a ketone.

definitions.definition: Organic compounds that have a carboxyl group (-COOH), which is a functional group containing a carbonyl group and a hydroxyl group.
definitions.example: Acetic acid (CH3COOH) is an example of a carboxylic acid.

definitions.definition: A functional group containing a carbon-oxygen double bond (C=O), which is present in aldehydes and ketones.
definitions.example: The carbonyl group is present in the structure of formaldehyde.

definitions.definition: Aldehydes that do not have a benzene ring in their structure.
definitions.example: Acetaldehyde (CH3CHO) is an example of an aliphatic aldehyde.

definitions.definition: Aldehydes that have a benzene ring in their structure.
definitions.example: Benzaldehyde (C6H5CHO) is an example of an aromatic aldehyde.

definitions.definition: Ketones that do not have a benzene ring in their structure.
definitions.example: Acetone (CH3COCH3) is an example of an aliphatic ketone.

definitions.definition: Ketones that have a benzene ring in their structure.
definitions.example: Acetophenone (C6H5COCH3) is an example of an aromatic ketone.

definitions.definition: A functional group containing a carbonyl group and a hydroxyl group (-COOH).
definitions.example: The carboxyl group is present in the structure of acetic acid.

definitions.definition: The ability of a carboxylic acid to donate a proton (H+ ion), which is a measure of its acidic strength.
definitions.example: Acetic acid is a weak acid and has a low acidic strength.

definitions.definition: A group of atoms that are bonded to a carbon atom, which is a part of a larger molecule.
definitions.example: The methyl group (CH3) is an example of an alkyl group.

definitions.definition: A group of atoms that are bonded to a benzene ring, which is a part of a larger molecule.
definitions.example: The benzene ring (C6H5) is an example of an aromatic group.

definitions.definition: A type of reaction in which a nucleophile (a species that donates a pair of electrons) adds to a carbonyl group.
definitions.example: The addition of water to an aldehyde or ketone is an example of nucleophilic addition.

definitions.definition: A species that donates a pair of electrons, which is involved in a nucleophilic addition reaction.
definitions.example: Water (H2O) is an example of a nucleophile.

definitions.definition: A species that accepts a pair of electrons, which is involved in an electrophilic addition reaction.
definitions.example: A hydrogen ion (H+) is an example of an electrophile.

definitions.definition: A type of reaction in which an electrophile adds to a double bond, resulting in the formation of a new bond.
definitions.example: The addition of hydrogen to an alkene is an example of electrophilic addition.

definitions.definition: A chemical reaction in which an aldehyde is converted to a carboxylic acid, resulting in the loss of hydrogen atoms.
definitions.example: The oxidation of formaldehyde to formic acid is an example of the oxidation of an aldehyde.

definitions.definition: A chemical reaction in which an aldehyde is converted to an alcohol, resulting in the gain of hydrogen atoms.
definitions.example: The reduction of formaldehyde to methanol is an example of the reduction of an aldehyde.

definitions.definition: A chemical reaction in which a ketone is converted to a carboxylic acid, resulting in the loss of hydrogen atoms.
definitions.example: The oxidation of acetone to acetic acid is an example of the oxidation of a ketone.

definitions.definition: A chemical reaction in which a ketone is converted to an alcohol, resulting in the gain of hydrogen atoms.
definitions.example: The reduction of acetone to isopropanol is an example of the reduction of a ketone.
